calibration.calibration_note: With hazard_harm=0.02, avg episode ~182 steps (was ~18 with 0.5). Actual episode time higher.

items.title: MECH-063 (ii) tonic/phasic dissociation -- step-denominated per-cell cap (supersedes 779a)
items.note: ONE-PARAMETER exposure fix for V3-EXQ-779a per failure_autopsy_MECH-063-777a-779a-cluster_2026-07-18 (REE_assembly origin/master 0a5cd3f24b), targets[1], routing=queue-experiment. 779a's LOAD-BEARING criterion double_dissociation_C1_and_C2 ALREADY PASSED -- criteria_non_degenerate true, robust true, diss_seed_count 4 = min_seeds 4, mean_dS_tonic +0.265 (sd 0.072) vs SUSTAINED_MARGIN 0.05, mean_dR_phasic -0.048 (sd 0.037) vs TRANSIENT_MARGIN 0.02 -- and was withheld SOLELY because the precondition phasic_fires_real_events measured 6 vs threshold 10, a MIN across cells sourced entirely from seed 23 / arm T1P1. ROOT CAUSE, capability RULED OUT (do not re-investigate SD-069): burst_level_max = 1.00 in EVERY PHASIC-ON cell including both seed-23 cells, and seed 23's firing RATE (7.2-11.9 event ticks per 1000 env steps) sits INSIDE the range other seeds span (seed 29: 8.8-9.6). Seed 23 dies in ~7 steps per episode, so 779a's 120-EPISODE cap bound before its 2400-STEP cap and the cell received only 835 of 2400 available steps; seed 23's four cells are the ONLY ones at/near the episode cap (n_episodes 116/116/120/120 vs 8-52 elsewhere) and its two PHASIC-ON cells the ONLY two of sixteen reporting stop_reason 'episode_cap'. 779a's F1 made the stopping RULE sample-driven but left the CAP episode-denominated, so the V3-EXQ-777/779 starvation defect reappeared one layer down on the same seed. THE FIX (one parameter, made structural): MAX_EPISODES_PER_CELL is now DERIVED from MAX_ENV_STEPS_PER_CELL (equal to it) instead of an independent 120, so the 2400-step cap binds for EVERY cell however short its episodes; a module-level assert pins the invariant against regression. This restores the shared helper's own documented default (RolloutBudget.__post_init__ sets max_episodes = max_env_steps when not overridden) -- 779a's 120 was an override of already-correct behaviour, so the whole 2x2 telemetry-probe family gets it right by default. Expected: seed 23 / T1P1 goes 835 -> 2400 steps, taking event ticks 6 -> ~17 at its own measured rate, clearing MIN_EVENT_TICKS 10 without touching the threshold. Progress prints are step-denominated to match (episodes_per_run 2400 = MAX_ENV_STEPS_PER_CELL; realised episodes per cell span 8 to ~343, so no episode denominator can describe both). DELIBERATELY UNCHANGED per the autopsy: (a) min-across-cells readiness aggregation -- it did LEGITIMATE work here and must NOT be relaxed, since unlike in V3-EXQ-779 the vetoing cell IS the criterion's largest contributor (seed 23 carries dR_phasic -0.108 on just 6 event ticks, and excluding it leaves 3 dissociating seeds vs min_seeds 4, so the PASS genuinely depended on the under-exposed cell); (b) the sample-vs-capability precondition kind classification (readiness_unmet_sample_kind / readiness_unmet_capability_kind), which is why 779's mislabelling did not recur; (c) per-cell stop_reason recording, which separated the one episode_cap cell from the fifteen step_cap cells and made this a one-command diagnosis. No threshold, margin or aggregation rule is nudged -- only exposure changes. EXPECTED OUTCOME: adjudication on this run; the open question is only whether seed 23's dR_phasic (-0.108) survives adequate exposure. GOV-REUSE-1: decisive readout = event ticks + dR_phasic for seed 23 / T1P1 at adequate exposure; checked v3_exq_779 and v3_exq_779a (the only runs of this family; 779a substrate_hash 765ce9d248) -- 779a records that cell at 835 of 2400 steps with 6 event ticks, i.e. it records the SHORTFALL not the answer, and no reprocessing can manufacture unobserved ticks -> run. Re-derive brake: claim-level count reaches threshold 2 but this lineage is EXPLICITLY EXEMPT (targets[1].re_derive_brake.exempt=true, refused_requeue=false, route_to=queue-experiment) -- it is not circling a substrate ceiling, its load-bearing criterion passed non-degenerately and robustly, and refusing 779b would discard a nearly-complete result rather than prevent a re-derive loop; the autopsy's recommended_substrate_queue_entry for this target is action 'none'. NOT blocked on SD-PROBE-WARMUP / SD-073, which brakes the 777 lineage (sub-claim i) only. Cloud affinity so the in-line OFF-arm mint stays linux-x86_64-py3.10 class (reusable by cloud consumers); the changed cap is declared in the fingerprint config_slice, so 779a's cells correctly refuse reuse. Sibling: V3-EXQ-777a (sub-claim i), which IS braked pending SD-073. Smoke: PASS (dry-run).
